AuditRecord.description: A single audit trail entry describing an action that occurred at a specific time.

action.description:
Action label describing what happened. Common values include:
* `sign` – A signature was performed.
* `email` – An email was sent.
* `payment` – A payment was successfully processed.
* `identification` – A verification or onboarding step was completed.
* `transaction` – The process completed (fully signed or discontinued).

createdDate.description: Timestamp when the record was created (UTC).

extras.description:
Additional action-specific data as key/value pairs.
Values may be primitives or small JSON objects depending on the action.
Example keys:
* `extras.sign_signatureLevel` : "QUALIFIED"
* `extras.email_type` : "INVITE"

id.description: Unique identifier of this audit record.

AuditSuccessRecordResponse.description: Response containing a list of auditing records for a process

records.description: Audit records ordered by creation date descending.

Error.description: Error object returned in case of an unsuccessful request.

clientBody.description: Detailed description of the error for client display.
clientBody.example: The requested organization could not be located. Please verify the ID.

clientSubject.description: Short subject/title of the error for client display.

httpStatus.description: HTTP status code of the error.

id.description: Unique identifier of this error for log correlation.

message.example: The requested organization does not exist.

name.description: Logical error name derived from the thrown exception class name (without 'Exception' suffix).

path.description: Path of the request that caused the error.

FileAttachment.description: Represents the configuration for attaching a PDF to a Process

content.description: Content of the PDF as Base64 (OpenAPI format: byte). Max 10 MiB raw.

contentType.description: MIME type of the uploaded file. Must be 'application/pdf'.

externalIdentifier.description: Optional client-defined identifier to reference this file.

signaturePositions.description: Optional signature positions to be applied to this PDF (write-only).

items.description: Optional signature positions to be applied to this PDF (write-only).

FileAttachResponse.description: Response object returned when a file is attached to a process.

fileId.description: System-assigned identifier of the attached file.

FileReference.description: Reference to an uploaded file with its versions and signature positions.

externalIdentifier.description: External reference identifier provided by the client system for correlation.

initialFileId.description: System-generated UUID of the initial file uploaded to the process.

lastSignedFileId.description: System-generated UUID of the last signed file (after the most recent signature).

signaturePositions.description: List of positions where signatures are placed in the file.

FileSignaturePosition.description: Represents the position of a signature in a file attachment.

pageNumber.description:
Zero-based index of the page. Negative values count backwards. Examples:
- `0` = first page
- `1` = second page
- `2` = third page
- ...
- `-2` = second to last page
- `-1` = last page

positionX.description: X-coordinate (in pixels) of the top-left corner of the signature field.

positionY.description: Y-coordinate (in pixels) of the top-left corner of the signature field.

signer.description:
Identifier of the signer. Either:
- the internal `identification` (UUID as string), e.g. `02c2f3cc-e263-40c9-b458-79c014a153a2`
- or the `externalIdentifier` from your system (string, up to 128 characters)

stickerProvider.description: Specifies which sticker renderer to use for this signature position. Default is VISUAL_SIGNATURE_PROVIDER.

Invitees.description:
Invitees represent the **additional** signers to be invited for this process.
The process `initiator` may optionally sign as well; if the initiator is a signer,
they are **not** listed here again.

personalMessage.description:
Personal message included in invitation emails.
Plain text only: HTML tags are stripped. Use \n for line breaks.
personalMessage.example: Hello,\nplease review and sign the documents. Thank you!

signers.description: Additional signers to invite (excluding the initiator if the initiator also signs).

items.description: Additional signers to invite (excluding the initiator if the initiator also signs).

externalIdentifier.description: External reference from your system for correlation.

name.description: Display name of the technical system.

NonPerson.description: Technical participant (non-human). The 'type' discriminator must be 'NON_PERSON'.
NonPerson.title: NonPerson

Notification.description:
Participant-specific notification instruction.

Fields:
- participant — reference to the participant (UUID or external identifier)
- notificationType — one or more events that trigger an email (INVITE, COMPLETION)
- language — optional BCP-47 language override for this participant

Notes:
- Supported languages: en-GB, de-CH, fr-CH, it-CH (BCP-47).

language.description: Optional language override for this participant (BCP-47).

notificationType.description: One or more notification types to send.

items.description:
The supported notification types:

* `INVITE` – Notifies the participant as soon as the process is released.
* `COMPLETION` – Notifies the participant once the process has been completed.

participant.description: Participant reference: either the system UUID (e.g. '02c2…') or the external identifier used at creation (e.g. 'crm-4711').

Notification.title: Notification

Notifications.description:
Optional container to control participant email notifications on release and/or completion.

Object semantics (when present):
- `language` is **required**.
- If `notifications` is **omitted**, all participants are notified using `language`.
- If `notifications` is **present**, it acts as a full override: **all participants must be listed**.
  Mixed modes (some overridden, some implicit) are not supported.

Notes:
- Supported languages: en-GB, de-CH, fr-CH, it-CH (BCP-47).

language.description: Default language for all notifications (overridable per entry). Supported values: `en-GB`, `de-CH`, `fr-CH`, `it-CH`. If not provided, the system defaults to `en-GB`.

notifications.description:
Full per-participant overrides. If present, **all participants must be listed**.
Leave this property **absent** (not empty array) if you want to notify everyone in the default `language`.

items.description:
Full per-participant overrides. If present, **all participants must be listed**.
Leave this property **absent** (not empty array) if you want to notify everyone in the default `language`.

Participant.description: Polymorphic participant. The 'type' discriminator selects the concrete shape.

Person.description: Human participant who does not sign. The 'type' discriminator must be 'PERSON'.
Person.title: Person

Process.description:
A process represents an end-to-end signing flow with 1..n signers over 1..n documents.
The initiator can optionally sign; additional signers are listed in `invitees`.

archivedOn.description: Actual time when archival happened, if archived (UTC).

authorization.description: Authorization requirements for accessing the process.

fileReferences.description: Uploaded files incl. visual signature positions (read-only).

items.description: Uploaded files incl. visual signature positions (read-only).

initiator.description:
Identifies the process initiator.
One of:
* NonPerson – technical/app initiator (no signature).
* Person – human initiator (no signature). Either the email address or the first and last name must be specified.
* Signer – initiator is also a signer.

invitees.description: Additional signers to invite (initiator not repeated here).

partnerId.description: Optional partner identifier. If provided, it must reference an existing partner.

payload.description: Optional opaque application payload for correlation/auditing. May contain a JSON string.

properties.description: Optional custom metadata as key/value pairs attached to the process. Keys must be unique. Maximum of 8 entries.

items.description: Optional custom metadata as key/value pairs attached to the process. Keys must be unique. Maximum of 8 entries.

status.description: Current lifecycle state of the process.

teamId.description: Optional identifier of an existing team to which the process will be assigned. The team must be created in the cockpit beforehand and the teamId can be retrieved there. If provided, the process will be visible to all members of the specified team. The team assignment cannot be modified after the process is created.

validUntil.description:
Expiration timestamp of the process. After this point in time,
the system automatically transitions the process status to `EXPIRED`.

ProcessAuthentication.description: INTERNAL ONLY: Authentication for the first-party namespace. Requests from other clients are ignored.

namespace.description: Logical namespace of the first-party application (restricted).

ProcessOpenResponse.description: Response with the deep link to open the process in Swisscom Sign UI.

url.description: Participant-specific URL to open the process.

ProcessSetupRequest.description: Request model for the setup endpoint of a signing process.

authentication.description: Authentication information (temporary App-only feature). Ignored for all other clients.

backUrl.description: Back URL: allows the user to return from the Swisscom Sign UI to the calling application.

language.description: Preferred UI language (BCP-47). Allowed: en-GB, de-CH, fr-CH, it-CH.

mode.description: Mode: defines the display mode of the process page.

scope.description: Scope: defines the scopes that are requested on the token.
scope.example: openid sswp:process:create sswp:process:execute sswp:process:manage sswp:process:read sswp:user:read sswp:user:write

successUrl.description: Success URL: called after signing is completed. The document ID is transferred via query parameter. Must be a GET endpoint that issues a redirect back to the original application.

ProcessSetupResponse.description: Represents the response for process setup.

url.description: URL to the Swisscom Sign UI for completing the setup of the process.

ProcessStatusResponse.description: Response object containing the status of a process.

status.description: Current lifecycle state of the process.

Property.description:
A single property (key/value) that can be attached to the process.
Keys must be unique within the process.

ReleasedParticipant.description: Participant-specific details returned on process release (e.g., permanent access URL).

externalIdentifier.description: External reference of the participant as provided by the client system.

url.description:
Permanent participant-specific URL to open the signing process.
The link can be embedded in e-mails or client applications.
When opened, the participant must still pass the regular
authorization/identification flow in the process.

ReleaseResponse.description: Response object returned after releasing a signing process. Includes the process identifier, its status, and participant-specific access URLs.

id.description: System-assigned identifier of the released process.

participants.description: Participant-specific information such as access URLs.

status.description: Current lifecycle status of the process after release.

Signer.description: Human participant who signs. The 'type' discriminator must be 'SIGNER'.

certificateMatch.description: Outcome of the comparison between the signer's declared full name and the name contained in the signing certificate. This field is populated only after the signer has successfully signed the process. If the signing was performed without authentication or using mobile OTP authentication, the value is set to `UNCHECKED`. Otherwise, the result is either `MATCH` or `MISMATCH`, depending on the comparison outcome.

certificateName.description: Full name extracted from the signing certificate. This field is populated only after the signer has successfully signed the process.It is absent if the signer has not signed yet or if the signing was performed without authentication or using mobile OTP authentication.

discontinuedOn.description: Timestamp when the signer has been discontinued (UTC). Only present if status is DISCONTINUED.

mobile.description:
Mobile number in E.164 format (no spaces or dashes).
- Authorization behavior: If a mobile number is provided, the fallback code authorization uses an SMS code.
- Signing behavior: If the number is a Swiss mobile (+41…), it will be preselected for ADVANCED signatures and cannot be changed in the UI.

role.description: Role or function of the signer.

signedOn.description: Timestamp when the signer completed signing (UTC). Only present if status is SIGNED.

verification.description:
Optional online identity verification requirement (GwG).
If provided when creating the process, the signer must complete an online identification step before signing.
Verification results (payload/files/timestamps) are generated by the system and are read-only.

Signer.title: Signer

Verification.description:
Online identity verification (GwG) for a signer.

Usage (input):
- To enforce GwG identification, include this object in the signer when creating the process.
  Presence of this object (even as an empty object) enables the online identification requirement.
- `residentPermit` configures the verification flow. If omitted, the system defaults to `true`.

Outcome (read-only):
- Pending: both `verifiedOn` and `rejectedOn` are null.
- Approved: `verifiedOn` is set and result artifacts may be available.
- Rejected: `rejectedOn` is set. In this case, typically only `payload` is present.

Result artifacts (read-only):
- `payload` contains the provider result as a JSON string (system integrators only).
- `files` may contain references to a PDF report and a ZIP bundle provided by the verification provider.
  Files can be downloaded via `GET /api/process/{processId}/file/{fileId}`.
  The PDF is additionally attached to the process as a document and must be signed together with the other documents.

files.description:
File references created by the verification provider (read-only), e.g. PDF report and/or ZIP bundle.
Download via `GET /api/process/{processId}/file/{fileId}`.

items.description: File identifier (UUID) provided by the verification provider.

payload.description:
Provider result payload as JSON string (read-only).
Present for approved or rejected verifications. Intended for system integrators.

processId.description: Verification process identifier at the provider (read-only).

rejectedOn.description: Timestamp when verification was rejected (UTC, read-only). Mutually exclusive with `verifiedOn`.

residentPermit.description:
Whether a Swiss residence permit is required for the signer.

Input parameter used to configure the online identification flow.
If omitted, the system defaults to `true`.

verifiedOn.description: Timestamp when verification was approved (UTC, read-only). Mutually exclusive with `rejectedOn`.

Verification.title: Verification

SwisscomSignOAuth2.description: OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials flow for accessing the Swisscom Sign Integration API.

scopes.sswp:process:create: Create, configure, and submit a new signature process including documents, invitees, and signature options.
scopes.sswp:process:read: Read process metadata or download associated documents.

x-scopes.sswp:process:create: Create, configure, and submit a new signature process.
x-scopes.sswp:process:read: Read process metadata or download associated documents.

info.description:
# Purpose The Swisscom Sign *Integration API* allows backend systems to create, configure, release, and monitor digital signing processes end-to-end, with minimal manual interaction. # Main Capabilities & Flow - Create a process (`POST /api/process`) - Retrieve process details (`GET /api/process/{processId}`) - Attach documents (`POST /api/process/{processId}/attach`) - Either complete the process via: - **Non-interactive release through API** → `POST /api/process/{processId}/release` - **Handover to Swisscom Sign UI** for completion → `POST /api/process/{processId}/setup` - Monitor progress and retrieve results (`GET /api/process/{processId}/status`, `/record`) - Download signed documents (`GET /api/process/{processId}/file/{fileId}`) - Generate user-specific deep links (`GET /api/process/{processId}/open/{personId}`) This represents the typical end-to-end workflow. # Example Scenarios The following examples illustrate typical integration patterns for Swisscom Sign processes. Each example describes the main intent, involved actors, and the corresponding API flow. ## Example 1 – System-initiated Process with Invitee **Summary** Start a signing process programmatically and invite a person to sign. **Description** A backend system creates a new signing process and defines a single signer (invitee). The invitee receives a personalized deep link to complete the signing in Swisscom Sign. This is the most common integration pattern for automated business workflows. **Typical flow** 1. POST /api/process → create process 2. POST /api/process/{id}/attach → attach document 3. POST /api/process/{id}/release → release process 4. POST /api/process/{id}/open/{personId} → generate deep link for invitee ## Example 2 – System-initiated process with invitee and online identification **Summary** Start a signing process programmatically and invite a person to sign, requiring online identification. **Description** A backend system creates a new signing process and defines a single signer (invitee). In addition to the basic invite flow, the signer is configured with online identification (GwG). The signer must successfully complete the identification step before being allowed to sign. If identification is approved, the process continues with signing. If identification is rejected, the process cannot be completed. Identification results and related artifacts are handled by the system and exposed read-only via the API. **Typical flow** 1. POST /api/process → create process (with verification on signer) 2. POST /api/process/{id}/attach → attach document 3. POST /api/process/{id}/release → release process 4. POST /api/process/{id}/open/{personId} → generate deep link for invitee ## Example 3 – Delegated Process on Behalf of a User **Summary** Create a signing process on behalf of another person and assign them as signer. **Description** A system or organization representative (e.g. service desk or backend job) initiates a process on behalf of a user. The initiator acts as a delegate, while the end user becomes the signer. This pattern is common in enterprise integrations where the business application acts in place of the employee but the signature itself must come from the individual. **Typical flow** 1. POST /api/process with initiator set to the delegated user 2. POST /api/process/{id}/attach → attach document(s) 3. POST /api/process/{id}/release → make process available to the signer 4. POST /api/process/{id}/open/{personId} → generate deep link for initiator ## Example 4 – Draft Process with Later Adjustments **Summary** Create a draft process and modify details before release. **Description** A process is created in draft mode without immediate release. The client can attach or replace documents, update signer details, or modify properties before finalizing the process. This scenario is suitable when human validation or business logic must occur before allowing the release. **Typical flow** 1. POST /api/process → create draft 2. POST /api/process/{id}/attach → attach document(s) (optional) 3. POST /api/process/{id}/setup → finalize process # Target Audience System-to-system integrations such as line-of-business applications, backend services, and jobs that need to trigger and manage signing processes programmatically. # Authentication & Authorization - Access via **OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials** (Bearer token) - Supported scopes: - `sswp:process:create` – create and release processes - `sswp:process:read` – retrieve process details, status, records, and files **Authorization model** All clients use the same token endpoint and audience. Authorization is enforced by: - **Scopes** requested/granted to the client - An **optional token claim** `organization_id` If the access token includes `organization_id`, the API restricts access to that specific organization. If the claim is absent, the token is treated as tenant-wide (subject to tenant policy and scopes). **Token example (decoded payload)** ```json { "iss": "https://iam.example.com/", "sub": "client-123", "aud": "swisscom-sign-system-api", "scope": "sswp:process:create sswp:process:read", "organization_id": "7f0b2f2a-1e6e-4b30-9f0c-1c9a9b3d1234", "exp": 1735660800, "iat": 1735657200 } ``` # Client Registration Two credential models are supported: 1. Tenant-wide credentials (issued by provider) – access across the entire tenant. 2. Organization-scoped credentials in the public Swisscom Sign tenant – created and managed by each organization, restricted to its context. # Conventions - JSON for requests/responses (file uploads as specified per endpoint) - Stable IDs, reusable across requests - Timestamps in UTC (ISO-8601) # Versioning & Changes Semantic versioning (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH). Relevant changes are documented in the changelog. **Compatibility notice** - The **v1 API** remains available and can still be used with the **existing credentials**. - For the **v2 API**, **new credentials** are required. - The v1 documentation is available here: [Swisscom Sign Integration API v1](https://api.ajila.io/blink/v1/documentation/#) **Change Log** | Version | Description | |------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | **2.9.0** | Added optional `teamId` on `Process`. | | **2.8.0** | Added optional `partnerId` to `Process` for enhanced partner management and tracking. | | **2.7.0** | Added optional `authorization` on `Process` as write-only. Defaults to CODE for backwards-compatability when no authorization value is provided. | | **2.6.0** | Removed `domicile` on `Verification`. | | **2.5.0** | Added new field `stickerProvider` to `FileSignaturePosition`. This new field determines how signature stickers are rendered. Currently, two choices are available, the classic `"VISUAL_SIGNATURE_PROVIDER"` for backwards-compatibility, and a new `"CUSTOM_VISUAL_SIGNATURE_PROVIDER"`. If `"CUSTOM_VISUAL_SIGNATURE_PROVIDER"` is selected, users can manually draw a signature, which will be incorporated into the sticker. | | **2.4.0** | Restricted email length to `254` characters. Restricted `externalIdentifier` characters to strengthen url-encoding conformity. | | **2.3.0** | Renamed `pairing` and its fields `pairing.userId` and `pairing.app` to `authentication`, `authentication.identity` and `authentication.namespace`. | | **2.2.0** | - Added `scope` and `mode` to Setup and Open requests.
- `Signer.firstName` and `Signer.lastName` are now mandatory.
- `NonPerson.name` is now mandatory.
- Added process wide `email` validation on `Participants`.
- Process creation now returns `Process` scheme.
- Fixed a bug where the `externalIdentifier` was not accepted in the Open request. | | **2.1.0** | Added `signer.status`, `signer.signedOn` and `signer.discontinuedOn` to `Signer` schema. | | **2.0.0** | Major refactoring:
- Endpoints moved from `/document` → `/api/process`.
- Removed `authenticationLevel` (all processes now require process-level authorization with **CODE**).
- Added `skipAuthorization` one-time code for secure deep link opening.
- Simplified `/setup` endpoint (removed unused options).
- Introduced `properties` as a dedicated list of key/value pairs.
- Added support for permanent vs. temporary config in `/open`.
- Enforced stricter schema definitions.
- Consistent `Error` object introduced. | | **1.12.0** | Added `residentPermit` to `VerificationOnline` schema. | | **1.11.0** | Updated the `/document/{id}/attach` response schema. | | **1.10.0** | Removed property `stepUploadVisible`. | | **1.9.0** | Added `PairingConfig` to Setup and Open requests, marked `userId` as deprecated. | | **1.8.0** | Added unit documentation for signature placement coordinates. | | **1.7.0** | Removed `initiatorPlacementOptional`. | | **1.6.0** | Concretized data types for unix timestamp format. | | **1.5.0** | Return URLs configurable in Setup request. | | **1.4.0** | Ability to sign different files from a subset of signers. | | **1.3.0** | Added `Properties` to the document. | | **1.2.0** | Added `/document/{id}/record` service. | | **1.1.0** | Deprecated `SignaturePosition` on `Signer`, moved it to `FileReference`. | | **1.0.0** | Initial version. | # Services
info.title: Swisscom Sign Integration API

x-changelog.1.4.0: Ability to sign different files from a subset of signers.
x-changelog.1.5.0: Return URLs configurable in Setup request.
x-changelog.1.6.0: Concretized data types for unix timestamp format.
x-changelog.1.8.0: Added unit documentation for signature placement coordinates.
x-changelog.1.9.0: Added `PairingConfig` to Setup and Open requests, marked `userId` as deprecated.
x-changelog.2.0.0:
Major refactoring:
- Endpoints moved from `/document` → `/api/process`.
- Removed `authentication` (all processes now require process-level authorization with CODE).
- Added `skipAuthorization` one-time code for secure deep link opening.
- Simplified `/setup` endpoint (removed unused options).
- Introduced `properties` as a dedicated list of key/value pairs.
- Added support for permanent vs. temporary config in `/open`.
- Enforced stricter schema definitions.
x-changelog.2.2.0:
- Added `scope` and `mode` to Setup and Open requests.
- `Signer.firstName` and `Signer.lastName` are now mandatory.
- `NonPerson.name` is now mandatory.
- Added process wide `email` validation on `Participants`.
- Process creation now returns `Process` scheme.
- Fixed a bug where the `externalIdentifier` was not accepted in the Open request.
x-changelog.2.3.0: Renamed `pairing` and its fields `pairing.userId` and `pairing.app` to `authentication`, `authentication.identity` and `authentication.namespace`.
x-changelog.2.4.0: Restricted email length to `254` characters. Restricted `externalIdentifier` characters to strengthen url-encoding conformity.
x-changelog.2.5.0: Added new field `stickerProvider` to `FileSignaturePosition`. This new field determines how signature stickers are rendered. Currently, two choices are available, the classic `VISUAL_SIGNATURE_PROVIDER` for backwards-compatibility, and a new `CUSTOM_VISUAL_SIGNATURE_PROVIDER`. If `CUSTOM_VISUAL_SIGNATURE_PROVIDER` is selected, users can manually draw a signature, which will be incorporated into the sticker.
x-changelog.2.7.0: Added optional `authorization` on `Process` as write-only. Defaults to CODE for backwards-compatability when no authorization value is provided.
x-changelog.2.8.0: Added optional `partnerId` to `Process` for enhanced partner management and tracking.

post.description:
Creates a new signing process.
- Non-interactive (API release): Provide all required data (documents, signers, options) up front.
- Setup (handover to Swisscom Sign UI): You can submit a minimal process and adjust metadata later in the UI.

(2) System-initiated process with invite.description: (2) System-initiated process with invitee and online identification

(3) Delegated process on behalf of a use.description: (3) Delegated process on behalf of a user

requestBody.description: Minimal payload for creating a signing process.

401.description: Authorization information is missing or invalid.

409.description: Occurs when there is a general error.

get.description:
Returns the full process object including participants, documents, properties,
and current status.

Can only be accessed if the process has been initiated and the caller is authorized.

401.description: Authorization information is missing or invalid.

409.description: Occurs when there is a general error.

post.description:
Attaches one or more PDF files to the specified process. You may repeat this call up to a maximum of 100 files.
Only `application/pdf` is supported, and each file must not exceed 10MB in size.

**Preconditions:**
- The process status must be `CREATED`.

parameters.description: ID of the process to which the PDF will be attached.
parameters.name: processId

(2) System-initiated process with invite.description: (2) System-initiated process with invitee and online identification

(3) Delegated process on behalf of a use.description: (3) Delegated process on behalf of a user

requestBody.description: PDF file and optional signature positions.

201.description: The file has been attached successfully.

401.description: Authorization information is missing or invalid.

409.description: Occurs when there is a general error.

get.description:
Retrieves a file that belongs to the specified process.

Possible file types:
- Attached original documents
- Signed documents (once the process status is COMPLETED)
- Verification documents (if online verification was performed)

Files are only available until the process has been archived (archivedOn is set).

parameters.description:
Identifier of the file within the process.

Can be either:
- The internal UUID assigned by the system
- The externalIdentifier provided during upload
parameters.name: fileId

200.description: Returns a file attached to the document.

401.description: Authorization information is missing or invalid.

409.description: Occurs when there is a general error.

410.description: The document process was archived and the file has been deleted.

412.description: The file cannot be downloaded yet because the document is not in the appropriate state.

post.description:
Generate an URL for a specific participant that can be used to open the process in the Swisscom Sign UI. Optional return URLs can be used to redirect the user back to the calling third party system. If an unreleased process is opened, an error is returned. For unreleased processes, /process/{processId}/setup can be used.

Preconditions:
* The service can only be used if the process has already been released via /processId/{processId}/release.

parameters.description: The system-assigned identifier or the external reference from your system can be used to identify the person.
parameters.name: personId

schema.description: The system-assigned identifier or the external reference from your system can be used to identify the person.

(2) Delegated process on behalf of a use.description: (2) Delegated process on behalf of a user (with authorization)

requestBody.description:
Defines how the Swisscom Sign UI should behave when the process is opened for a person.
Typical use cases:
- Control the language of the UI.
- Configure redirect URLs (back, success).
- Skip authorization for trusted environments.
- Provide pairing information (App-only, temporary).

200.description: You receive an URL specific to the subscriber with a defined validity period.

401.description: Authorization information is missing or invalid.

409.description: Occurs when there is a general error.

get.description: This service can be used only with a valid process. All created auditing records are returned in descending order of creation date. Thus, the first record is the most recent.

200.description: Returns a list of auditing records.

401.description: Authorization information is missing or invalid.

409.description: Occurs when there is a general error or the process is missing.

post.description:
Releases a previously created and fully configured process. Personal access URLs are returned for each participant.
These can be used for asynchronous execution, typically via email.

Optionally, you may include email parameters to notify participants directly.

Preconditions:
- A process was created via `/process` with at least one participant.
- At least one document was attached using `/process/{processId}/attach`.
- Each document includes a configured position for a visual signature.
- All participants have a visual signature assigned.
- All visual signatures are placed within the visible area.

parameters.description: Unique identifier of the process to be released.
parameters.name: processId

(1a) System-initiated process with invit.description: (1a) System-initiated process with invitee (emails in German)

(2) System-initiated process with invite.description: (2) System-initiated process with invitee and online identification (emails in German)

(3) Delegated process on behalf of a use.description: (3) Delegated process on behalf of a user (emails in German)

requestBody.description:
Optional notification configuration.

Call semantics:
- No body → the system sends **no notifications**.
- Body with only `language` → all participants are notified in that language.
- Body with `language` and `notifications` → full per-participant override; when one participant is overridden, **all participants must be listed**.

200.description: Process released; participant URLs returned (permanently valid).

208.description: Process was already released; participant URLs returned (permanently valid).

401.description: Authorization information is missing or invalid.

403.description: Forbidden – caller not allowed to release this process.

post.description:
Launches the Swisscom Sign web interface for the initiator to complete the process setup.
This includes defining visual elements and participant data.

Once setup is complete, the process is automatically released and notifications are handled by Swisscom Sign.

**Preconditions:**
- A process was created via `/process`.
- The process has not yet been released.

parameters.description: Unique identifier of the process to finalize via UI.
parameters.name: processId

requestBody.description:
Defines how the Swisscom Sign UI should behave when the process is opened for setup.
Typical use cases:
- Control the language of the UI.
- Configure redirect URLs (back, success).
- Provide pairing information (App-only, temporary).

202.description: The setup request has been accepted. Please redirect to the provided URL.

401.description: Authorization information is missing or invalid.

409.description: Occurs when there is a general error.

post.summary: Finalize process in Swisscom Sign UI

get.description: Returns the current status of the process. Requires that the process exists and the caller is authorized.

200.description: Returns the state of the desired document.

401.description: Authorization information is missing or invalid.

409.description: Occurs when there is a general error.

tags.description: Allows you to set up your process according to your needs, including adding documents, configuring invitees, setting signature options, and submitting the process for execution.
tags.name: Process: create

tags.description: Access process information and download signed or original documents once the signing is complete.
tags.name: Process: read
